fields.title: memory repair path: fix stale update/agent loop behavior
fields.description: The redesigned memory summary/content contract required repairing legacy JSONL entries with summary == content. Rush agents got stuck in repetitive memory update loops without converging, and manual in-session memory update behavior appeared stale or skipped queued writes. Repair was completed by editing the JSONL files directly, but tau should provide a reliable repair path.nnObserved symptoms:n- rush agents repeated memory update calls on the same entriesn- some manual tool calls were skipped with 'Skipped due to queued user message.'n- at least one successful memory update response still rendered the old identical summary/content, suggesting stale tool/runtime behaviornnDefinition of done:n- reproduce the stale/looping repair behaviorn- identify whether the cause is tool runtime staleness, renderer staleness, session/tool registration, or agent promptingn- ensure manual memory update reliably persists and renders repaired summariesn- ensure agent-driven repair converges instead of loopingn- add regression coverage where practical
